stage1.reason: Best validation recall@10 among tested provider combinations while keeping strong ndcg@10 and candidate recall.

stage2.reason: Beat both the selected Stage 1 baseline and the v1 deterministic reranker on validation and test.

stage2_legacy_fallback.reason: Former promoted v1 ranker. Retained as a fallback and comparison baseline after LightGBM promotion.

mmr.reason: Category one-hot MMR failed the diversity gate and remains flag-only.

phase5_kure_mmr.reason: KURE dense embedding re-evaluation was blocked on validation at all lambdas (0.5,0.7,0.8,0.9); default MMR behavior unchanged.

phase2_5_default.reason: Regularization tuning promoted num_leaves=31. Negative sampling and source one-hot ablations did not beat the current default. Category one-hot MMR remains no-go.

accepted_components.name: popularity
accepted_components.notes: Strong standalone baseline and fallback source.

accepted_components.name: cooccurrence
accepted_components.notes: Best collaborative-style provider in the canonical item space.

accepted_components.name: stage2_reranker
accepted_components.notes: Former default v1 deterministic reranker. Retained as a fallback and historical comparison point.

accepted_components.name: stage2_lightgbm_ranker
accepted_components.notes: LightGBM binary classifier. AUC=0.8890555966387075, best_iteration=84. Beats v1 on both validation and test. Top features: cooccurrence_score, popularity_prior.

auxiliary_components.name: lightgcn
auxiliary_components.notes: Canonicalization revived LightGCN, but merging it into the default Stage 1 baseline reduced validation recall.

rejected_or_disabled_components.name: segment_popularity
rejected_or_disabled_components.reason: Toxic in ablation. Strongly degrades recall and ndcg.

rejected_or_disabled_components.name: bm25_itemknn
rejected_or_disabled_components.reason: Did not beat raw cooccurrence or the selected Stage 1 baseline.

rejected_or_disabled_components.name: idf_cooccurrence
rejected_or_disabled_components.reason: Close to baseline but still slightly worse.

rejected_or_disabled_components.name: pop_capped_cooccurrence
rejected_or_disabled_components.reason: Close to baseline but still slightly worse.

rejected_or_disabled_components.name: lightgcn_merge
rejected_or_disabled_components.reason: Merging LightGCN with popularity + cooccurrence lowered validation recall.

rejected_or_disabled_components.name: mmr_category_onehot
rejected_or_disabled_components.reason: Phase 3 NO-GO: Category one-hot embeddings produce binary cosine similarity (0 or 1), making MMR lambda sweep ineffective. All lambda values (0.1-0.9) produce identical results to v2 baseline. Diversity gate failed (0/3 metrics improved). KURE dense embeddings needed for MMR re-evaluation.

rejected_or_disabled_components.name: mmr_kure_dense_embeddings
rejected_or_disabled_components.reason: Phase 5-A KURE dense embedding MMR sweep (λ=0.5/0.7/0.8/0.9) improved several diversity metrics in one case but failed accuracy gates on every candidate; no test execution required.

rejected_or_disabled_components.name: g7_9_diversity_novelty_weights
rejected_or_disabled_components.reason: Implemented but default disabled. Accuracy mostly held, but global coverage, novelty, and long-tail representation regressed under the diversity gate.

rejected_or_disabled_components.name: source_onehot_features
rejected_or_disabled_components.reason: Validation recall and ndcg were below the current no-source-feature default, so test evaluation was skipped. source_count had gain, but individual source flags had zero gain under the current popularity+cooccurrence candidate pool.

data_findings.finding: Canonicalization materially improved graph density and made LightGCN viable again, but over-merged canonical hobbies remain a qualitative risk.
Stage 1 candidate generation remains popularity-heavy. Stage 2 reduces this bias but does not fully remove repetitive top recommendations.

Category one-hot embeddings produce binary cosine similarity (0 or 1), making MMR lambda sweep a no-op. Within the candidate pool top-k, most items share the same dominant category, so MMR cannot diversify within-category. Continuous embeddings (KURE) are needed for meaningful MMR reordering.

KURE dense embedding MMR with λ=0.5,0.7,0.8,0.9 improved recall/ndcg variance vs lambda but could not beat the closed Phase 2.5 baseline on validation recall or NDCG. Coverage and novelty gains at λ=0.5 were offset by recall/NDCG misses; no winner selected.

LightGBM ranker improves accuracy (recall@10 +0.0450, ndcg@10 +0.0225 on validation vs Stage 1 baseline) but reduces diversity (coverage@10: 0.1556 vs v1=0.517, novelty@10: 4.584 vs v1=4.732). The learned ranker concentrates scores on popular items — this is a ranking collapse problem, NOT a retrieval bottleneck. Evidence: candidate_recall@50=97.8% (sufficient pool), yet v2 LightGBM coverage@10=15.56% vs v1 deterministic coverage@10=51.7% on the SAME pool. The model learned to preferentially rank popular hobbies, collapsing top-k diversity.

Feature importance is heavily concentrated: cooccurrence_score (60.73%) + popularity_prior (28.14%) = 88.87% of total gain. The remaining features contribute a small fraction; this reflects learned concentration under the current regularized config (num_leaves=31, min_data_in_leaf=50, reg_alpha=0.1, reg_lambda=0.1), not zero usefulness. 4 features remain zero because they are constant by design (popularity_penalty=0.0, category_diversity_reward=0.0) or disabled (lightgcn_score/segment_popularity_score remain unhelpful here).

phase2_5_regularization_partial.decision: Promoted candidate (validation and test fixed-config completed)
phase2_5_regularization_partial.reason: Targeted validation tuning found better num_leaves=31. Fixed-config follow-up completed on validation and test, passing both gates.
phase2_5_regularization_partial.next_step: Pin num_leaves=31 if the team approves default hyperparameter update.

phase2_5_negative_sampling.reason: Validation favored hard_ratio=1.0, but the single final test check underperformed the current hard_ratio=0.8 default on Recall@10 and NDCG@10. Keep current default and do not promote the negative-sampling change.

feature_importance_observation.interpretation: The merged candidate pool already carries source score information; explicit source flags did not improve ranking under the current provider set.

phase2_5_source_onehot_ablation.next_step: Close Phase 2.5 default decision or proceed to KURE/text embedding experiments with leakage gates.

phase5_kure_mmr.reason: All Phase 5-A lambdas failed the accuracy gate (delta recall and delta NDCG both below threshold), so test execution was skipped and default is unchanged.
phase5_kure_mmr.decision_summary: keep default MMR=false and selected LightGBM ranker baseline

phase2_5_default_decision_closure.next_experiment_baseline_rule: Future experiments must compare against this closed Phase 2.5 default unless explicitly documented otherwise. Select on validation only and run test once for the selected validation winner.
phase2_5_default_decision_closure.next_step: Use this default as the fixed baseline for KURE dense embedding MMR or leakage-safe text embedding experiments.

phase3_mmr_diversity.reason: MMR with category one-hot embedding failed diversity gate. All lambda values (0.1-0.9) produced identical results to v2 baseline because one-hot embeddings make cosine similarity only 0 or 1, so MMR cannot reorder within same-category items.

mmr_sweep_lambda_0.1_to_0.9.note: ALL lambda values produced identical metrics to v2 baseline. One-hot embeddings make MMR a no-op within top-k.

diversity_gate.accuracy_gate: PASS (recall drop within tolerance for most lambda)
diversity_gate.diversity_metrics_improved: 0 out of 3 (coverage DOWN, novelty DOWN, ILD marginal)
diversity_gate.verdict: NO-GO - category one-hot embedding too coarse for MMR to diversify within candidate pool

phase3_mmr_diversity.root_cause_analysis: Category one-hot embeddings produce cosine similarity = 1.0 for same-category items and 0.0 for different-category items. Within the top-k candidate list, most items belong to the same dominant category, so MMR similarity penalty becomes either 0 or 1. Lambda sweep has no effect because the similarity matrix is binary. KURE dense embeddings (Phase 5 planned) would fix this by providing continuous similarity.
phase3_mmr_diversity.decision: Learned ranker only (Phase 2 result). MMR available as --use-mmr flag (default false). KURE embedding integration deferred to Phase 5 for MMR re-evaluation.
next_priorities: 1. LightGBM regularization tuning (small data, overfitting risk)
4. KURE embedding cache (Phase 5) for MMR re-evaluation
